lines: The rope hangs slack in your hand.
Above the ceiling, the bell has already spent its warning.
The rope hangs alone in a narrow bell chamber northeast of the nave.
Its upper length vanishes into a square of black ceiling. The old bell above it cannot be seen, only guessed by the weight in the rope.
One pull will tell the Remnant that Ash Chapel can be entered.

effects.log: You haul the bell rope. High above the ceiling, the old metal answers.

questUpdate.log: Quest updated: the Remnant will hear the chapel bell. The Hallowfen checkpoints still have not answered.

showBriefing.title: ACT I: THE HALLOWFEN
pages: The rope bites into your hands. Above the ceiling, iron wakes after years of dust.
The sound leaves Ash Chapel in slow waves. It crosses the broken garden, the wall road, the catacomb stair, every room where someone is trying not to listen.
The Remnant will know the chapel is safe enough to enter. Your writ is not finished.
You broke one cell, not the hand that sent it here.
The Hallowfen checkpoints stopped answering before the Choir reached this house. No bell from the wall. No runner on the road. No mistake large enough to be innocent.
Follow the Hallowfen road. Find who silenced the towers.

page: Below, Selka starts counting people by name instead of by hiding place. The broom in her hands sounds too small for the work, and still she keeps at it.
One child asks whether safe means forever. No one lies quickly enough.
Corin keeps both arms around the blue ball, as if color itself might need guarding.
Eda watches from the chalk marks and tries to look bored instead of relieved.
Mirel's tin saint rides in your pack, warm from her hand.
Jarik went toward Hallowfen for wall pay. If he is alive, the road has him.
The cross behind the altar no longer whispers.
Mercy made no speech when it left him, only weight on the stone and a silence that did not feel clean.
In the cellar, the bound guard does not have to learn the cult's next word.
The post stands empty in the cold.

lines: The rope hangs alone in a narrow bell chamber northeast of the nave.
If the bell rings now, it calls people into a chapel that still has knives in it.
